preamble: The RUKI rule is a historical sound change in the Indo-Iranian branch of Indo-European, where an original /s/ becomes retroflex [ʂ] (or palatal [ʃ] in some branches) when following /r/, /u/, /k/, or /i/.

examples.note: Historical /s/ > [ʂ] after r, u, k, i (e.g., in verbal prefixes and nominal endings).

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
